payload.title: Audit Committee Book
payload.description: Audit Committee Meeting book for January 21, 2021, containing meeting agenda, minutes from October 28, 2020, 2021 work plan, Q3 2020 financial statements, Enterprise Risk Management and Internal Control Guidelines, 2020 audit plan status and 2021 proposed audit plan, DDCR performance measures, and open audit recommendations.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: 2020 Audit Activity through Q3 - Operational Audits Completed
tool_args.raw_text: Through September, MTA Audit has completed 51 of 104 planned operational audits and 46 audits were in progress.

payload.description: Operational audits completed through Q3 2020

tool_args.label: 2020 Audit - Potential Savings Identified
tool_args.raw_text: These audits identified potential savings and efficiency opportunities of $10.8 million.

tool_args.label: 2020 Audit - Capital Project Audits
tool_args.raw_text: There were also 218 audits performed relating to capital projects.

payload.raw_value_text: 218 audits performed relating to capital projects

tool_args.label: 2020 Third-Party Questioned Costs
tool_args.raw_text: Third-party questioned costs were $38.4 million and past experience has demonstrated an 87% recovery rate for questioned costs.

tool_args.label: Questioned Cost Recovery Rate
tool_args.raw_text: past experience has demonstrated an 87% recovery rate for questioned costs

tool_args.label: Pension QA Group - 2020 Review Results
tool_args.raw_text: To date, the Pension QA Group has reviewed 1,100 pension calculations and has identified 214 errors with an estimated impact of $3.7 million in those calculations.

tool_args.label: Pension QA - Errors with Financial Impact

payload.raw_value_text: identified 214 errors with an estimated impact of $3.7 million
payload.description: Errors identified in pension calculations by the Pension QA Group

payload.raw_value_text: reviewed 1,470 pension files from January 2020 through December 2020
Total cost impact over the expected life of retiree $4.1M
payload.description: Total cost impact over the expected life of retiree from pension calculation errors

payload.raw_value_text: Projects Completed 79 Findings with Recommendations 165 Savings/Cost Efficiencies $12.5 M
Projects Completed 278 Pre-Award 16 Overhead Reviews 159 Contract Close-outs 96 Claims 2 $ Audited $762.5 M Questioned Costs $69.7 M

payload.raw_value_text: Total Grant Expenditures $3.14 Billion Costs Audited $521.2 Million Projects/Recommendations 135/365 Follow-up Audits Completed 11 Total Cost Adjustments $62 Million

payload.scope: All MTA agencies (MNR: 4, LIRR: 1 open significant deficiencies)
payload.description: Open significant deficiencies as of January 2021 across MTA agencies
Decline in preliminary MTA system-wide utilization through the third quarter of 2020 relative to 2019

payload.raw_value_text: MTA New York City Transit subway ridership declined by 757.5 million trips (60.0%)
payload.scope: MTA New York City Transit subway

payload.raw_value_text: MTA New York City Transit bus declined by 277.6 million trips (66.4%)
payload.scope: MTA New York City Transit bus

payload.raw_value_text: MTA Long Island Rail Road ridership declined by 43.7 million trips (64.2%)
MTA Metro-North Railroad declined by 42.3 million trips (65.5%)
MTA Bus declined by 59.3 million trips (65.7%)
MTA Staten Island Railway declined by 2.1 million trips (63.6%)
Vehicle traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities through the third quarter decreased by 62.1 million crossings (25.1%)

tool_args.label: Deloitte Appointed as MTA External Auditor

payload.claim_text: A motion was made and seconded to approve Deloitte as the MTA's external auditors.
payload.description: Audit Committee approved the reappointment of Deloitte as MTA's external auditors at the October 28, 2020 meeting.

tool_args.label: Rear-door boarding and fare-free bus entry

payload.claim_text: From March 20, 2020 through the end of August, entry onto most buses was only permitted through the rear door only, and fares were not collected, and in these instances ridership was not counted.
payload.description: Rear-door boarding and fare-free bus entry was implemented during COVID-19 pandemic, affecting ridership counting.

payload.claim_text: The 2019 New York State Budget approved congestion pricing in Manhattan south of 60th Street, which is scheduled to go into effect in 2021 but awaits approval by the Federal Highway Administration to proceed.
payload.description: Congestion pricing approved in 2019 NYS Budget, scheduled to go into effect in 2021 pending FHWA approval.

payload.claim_text: For the MTA, the CARES Act provided $4.0 billion in funding, which has assisted in covering operating losses through late July.
payload.description: CARES Act provided $4 billion to MTA to cover operating losses.
